title: eriogonum latifolium subsp. saxicola
intro: Eriogonum latifolium subsp. saxicola, also known as the rocky buckwheat or rocky mountain buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory qualities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Eriogonum latifolium subsp. saxicola can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
